pullRequest.title: Dockerfile change to multi-stage with 'nettacker' as entrypoint  + related CI/CD changes
pullRequest.body:
This PR introduces a multi-stage Dockerfile to improve our container build process. The multi-stage approach separates dependency installation, application build, and runtime into distinct stages, resulting in a smaller and more efficient production image. Key benefit of this change:

- Reduced image size by excluding build tools and unnecessary files from the final runtime image (**50%+ image size reduction**)

Additionally, this PR updates the Dockerfile to set `ENTRYPOINT ["nettacker"]` . As a result, all Docker run commands now invoke `nettacker` directly (e.g., `docker run nettacker --show-all-modules` instead of the old `docker run poetry run python nettacker.py --show-all-modules`). This aligns Docker behaviour with common best practices for CLI tools (note poetry is no longer in the final runtime image to save the space )

Explanation of changes in `ci_cd.yaml`:

 - Replacement of `poetry run python nettacker.py` with just `nettacker`
 - Replacement of `127.0.0.1` with `host.docker.internal` because due to the entrypoint change `127.0.0.1` now means the container itself and not the host 
 - Removal of the `docker run pip install .` step as the nettacker package is installed as a wheel in Dockerfile's runtime stage
 
 

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
  * Improved Docker image build process with multi-stage builds for smaller size and better caching.
  * Updated container startup to use the installed CLI command by default.
  * Enhanced test execution in CI with simplified commands and improved host networking support.
  * Removed redundant installation steps during Docker image testing.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: Fix #1147: Add SMB signature to port.yaml
pullRequest.body:
---

<!--
  Thanks for contributing to OWASP Nettacker!
-->

## Proposed change

Added an SMB service-discovery signature to `port.yaml`.

This prevents a crash in `Module.load()` when the SMB module is executed.
Previously, if an SMB port (e.g., 445) was detected during `port_scan` but no SMB-specific signature existed, the code attempted to access `self.discovered_services["smb"]`, which did not exist — causing a **KeyError**.

After adding this minimal detection signature, `self.discovered_services["smb"]` is properly created whenever SMB services are present, preventing the crash.
I have verified this fix manually by running a local SMB server and scanning it — the scan now completes successfully without any crash.

**Fixes #1147**

---

## Type of change

* [x] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

---

* [x] Verified manually that scanning a live SMB server works correctly without errors.

[contributing-guidelines]: https://nettacker.readthedocs.io/en/latest/Developers/

pullRequest.title: Fix header matcher key to headers in modules ( Fix #1241)
pullRequest.body:
## Proposed change

This PR fixes incorrect usage of the `header` response matcher key in existing modules by replacing it with the supported `headers` key.
This prevents silent false positives caused by header conditions being ignored by the HTTP matcher logic (Fixes #1241).

help key.

- Updates the timeout argument to use the correct help string

No behavioral changes introduced.
Closes #1299

pullRequest.title: fix(api): disable reloader in debug mode to prevent macOS spawn crash
pullRequest.body: ## Proposed change The API service fails to start with the `--api-debug-mode` option on MacOS with Python 3.12. Add `use_reloader=False` to prevent Python from spawning a child process via `multiprocessing.spawn` when debug mode is on. The API service starts correctly with the fix. Closes #1313 ## Type of change - [ ] New core framework functionality - [x] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] Code refactoring without any functionality changes - [ ] New or existing module/payload change - [ ] Documentation/localization improvement - [ ] Test coverage improvement - [ ] Dependency upgrade - [ ] Other improvement (best practice, cleanup, optimization, etc) ## Checklist - [x] I've followed the [contributing guidelines][contributing-guidelines] - [x] I have **digitally signed** all my commits in this PR - [x] I've run `make pre-commit` and confirm it didn't generate any warnings/changes - [x] I've run `make test`, I confirm all tests passed locally - [ ] I've added/updated any relevant documentation in the `docs/` folder - [x] I've linked this PR with an open issue - [x] I've tested and verified that my code works as intended and resolves the issue as described - [x] I have attached screenshots demonstrating my code works as intended - [x] I've checked all other open PRs to avoid submitting duplicate work - [x] I confirm that the code and comments in this PR are not direct unreviewed outputs of AI - [x] I confirm that I am the Sole Responsible Author for every line of code, comment, and design decision [contributing-guidelines]: https://nettacker.readthedocs.io/en/latest/Developers/

pullRequest.title: Added a New Vulnerability Detection Module for CVE-2024-36401
pullRequest.body: ## Proposed change CVE-2024-36401 is a critical vulnerability in GeoServer, an opensource server that allows user to edit and share geospatial data. The Geoserver library API passes property names as common-jsxpath library which can execute arbitrary code when processing XPath expressions. Testing: - For vulnerable server, I used a vulnerable docker image of geoserver `v. 2.23.5`. - For non-vulnerable serve, I used geoserver `v.2.28.0`. ## Type of change - [ ] New core framework functionality - [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] Code refactoring without any functionality changes - [X] New or existing module/payload change - [ ] Documentation/localization improvement - [ ] Test coverage improvement - [ ] Dependency upgrade - [ ] Other improvement (best practice, cleanup, optimization, etc) ## Checklist - [X] I've followed the [contributing guidelines][contributing-guidelines] - [X] I have **digitally signed** all my commits in this PR - [X] I've run `make pre-commit` and confirm it didn't generate any warnings/changes - [X] I've run `make test`, I confirm all tests passed locally - [X] I've added/updated any relevant documentation in the `docs/` folder - [ ] I've linked this PR with an open issue - [X] I've tested and verified that my code works as intended and resolves the issue as described - [X] I have attached screenshots demonstrating my code works as intended - [X] I've checked all other open PRs to avoid submitting duplicate work - [x] I confirm that the code and comments in this PR are not direct unreviewed outputs of AI - [x] I confirm that I am the Sole Responsible Author for every line of code, comment, and design decision [contributing-guidelines]: https://nettacker.readthedocs.io/en/latest/Developers/

pullRequest.title: Fix error exclude all
pullRequest.body:
Description:-
Running `nettacker -i <target> -m <module> -x all` causes an unhandled KeyError crash instead of displaying a clean error message to the user.

Root Cause
arg_parser.py:658 calls die_failure(_("error_exclude_all"))`when the user passes -x all, but the key error_exclude_all is not defined in any locale file. This causes `messages.py:71` to raise a KeyError before the intended error message can be displayed.

Reproduction Steps
nettacker -i 127.0.0.1 -m port_scan -x all

Expected: A clean error message telling the user they cannot exclude all modules.
Actual: Unhandled KeyError: 'error_exclude_all' .

Fix
Add the missing key to nettacker/locale/en.yaml:
error_exclude_all: "Cannot exclude all modules. Please specify individual module names to exclude using -x."
 No changes to logic or behaviour  arg_parser.py:658 is already correct, only the locale key was missing.
 Other locale files can be updated by their respective translators in follow-up PRs.

fixes #1327

pullRequest.title: Fix mutable default argument in merge_logs_to_list
pullRequest.body:
## Proposed change

Replace `log_list=[]` with `log_list=None` and initialize inside the function body in `merge_logs_to_list()` (`nettacker/core/utils/common.py`).

The mutable default list was shared across all calls. Since it's mutated via `.append()` on line 41, log entries from previous invocations leak into subsequent results and the list grows without bound.

Linked issue: #1464

## Type of change
